title: Council Tax Reduction (pension age)
jurisdiction: England, pension age — these rules are nationally prescribed and every council's scheme must include them; Wales and Scotland run close-cousin national schemes

effective.uprating: The Council Tax Reduction Schemes (Prescribed Requirements) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 (S.I. 2026/27)

plainly: Help with the council tax bill, claimed through your council — but for pension-age households the rules are national law, so they compute here: on Guarantee Credit the bill is normally covered in full, and otherwise 20p of each £1 of spare income comes off the help.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to work out whether you have reached State Pension age — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 reg 3 (these prescribed rules apply to pensioners; working-age reduction is each council's own scheme)

questions.label: Do you live in Scotland?
questions.rule: Scotland runs its own national Council Tax Reduction scheme

questions.label: Do you live with a partner (married, civil partner, or as a couple)?
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885: a couple's income, capital and applicable amount are assessed together

questions.label: Your partner's date of birth
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 reg 3: a mixed-age couple is treated under the council's working-age scheme unless protected

questions.label: If one of you is under State Pension age: do you get pension-age Housing Benefit as a couple?
questions.help: Couples protected since before 15 May 2019. If unsure, answer no.

questions.label: Your weekly council tax — the annual bill for your home, after any discounts, divided by 52
questions.help: Use the bill after discounts such as the 25% single-person discount. Enter 0 if you have not looked it up yet.
questions.rule: Local Government Finance Act 1992 s.13A; SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 7 (the maximum reduction is set from the liability)

questions.label: Do you — or a partner you live with — get Pension Credit?
questions.help: If the Pension Credit answer above says you appear eligible, claim that first: on Guarantee Credit your income and savings are disregarded here entirely.
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 13 (guarantee credit: the whole of the applicant's capital and income must be disregarded)

questions.label: Your weekly State Pension (and your partner's, added together)
Weekly income from workplace or private pensions and annuities (both of you)
Weekly earnings from work, after tax (both of you)
questions.help: Enter 0 if none. A small amount is disregarded — the working-out below shows it.
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 Part 6 and its earnings disregards

questions.label: Other weekly income that counts — for example Carer's Allowance or Working Tax Credit (both of you)
questions.help: Do NOT include Attendance Allowance, DLA, PIP, Pension Age Disability Payment or Housing Benefit. Enter 0 if none.

questions.label: Total savings and investments (both of you) — not your home
questions.help: Over £16,000 normally ends the help — unless you get Guarantee Credit, when savings are disregarded entirely.
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 37 (tariff income: £1 per £500 above £10,000, up to the £16,000 ceiling); para 13 (guarantee credit exception)

questions.label: Do you get Attendance Allowance, the daily-living part of PIP or ADP, the middle or higher care rate of DLA, or Pension Age Disability Payment?
Does your partner get one of those benefits too?
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 2 (a couple's severe disability premium needs both to qualify)

questions.label: Apart from your partner, do any other adults (18 or over) normally live with you?
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 8 (non-dependant deductions) and Sch 2 (severe disability premium)

questions.label: Does anyone get Carer's Allowance (or Scotland's carer support payment) for looking after you or your partner?
questions.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 2 (no severe disability premium while a carer is paid for the care)

questions.label: Are you entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment) for looking after someone else?
questions.help: Answer yes even if it is not actually paid because of your State Pension (an “underlying entitlement” still counts).

questions.label: Is your partner entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment)?

values.plainly: Your applicable amount: the personal allowance (2026–27: £238.00 single, £363.25 couple) plus any premiums — the same shapes as Pension Credit's minimum guarantee.
values.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 2 Parts 1 and 3–4, as uprated from April 2026 by S.I. 2026/27

values.plainly: Earnings ignored each week: £20 where a carer premium applies, otherwise £10 for a couple, £5 single.
values.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 Part 6 earnings disregards (the pension-age standard amounts)

values.plainly: Deemed weekly income from savings above £10,000: £1 per £500 or part.
Income that counts — or nothing at all on Guarantee Credit, where the whole of your income and capital must be disregarded.
values.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 13 (guarantee credit); Part 6 otherwise

values.plainly: Income above your applicable amount (nil on Guarantee Credit).

The taper: 20p of help is withdrawn for each £1 of excess income. (The statute writes it daily — 2 6/7 per cent of the weekly excess against the daily bill — which is the same thing weekly; rounded up to the next penny here, up to 1p a week conservative.)
Council Tax Reduction: your weekly bill, less the taper.

gates.because: Scotland runs its own national Council Tax Reduction scheme, claimed through your council — close-cousin rules, but not the ones encoded here.
Under State Pension age, Council Tax Reduction is your council's own working-age scheme — genuinely local rules this page cannot compute. Apply through your council anyway; the help is real.
gates.rule: Local Government Finance Act 1992 s.13A(2); SI 2012/2885 reg 3 (the prescribed rules cover pensioners only)

gates.because: A couple with one partner under State Pension age is treated under the council's working-age scheme — local rules this page cannot compute. Apply through your council anyway.
Savings over £16,000 end the help — unless you get Guarantee Credit, when savings are disregarded entirely. If the Pension Credit answer above applies, claim it first and this gate disappears.
gates.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 37 (the £16,000 ceiling) and para 13 (the guarantee credit exception)

verdicts.because: On Pension Credit your income and savings are disregarded entirely: maximum reduction — normally the whole bill. Claim through your council (often the same form as Housing Benefit).

On Pension Credit you get maximum reduction — but a deduction applies for each other adult living with you, sized by their income (not encoded here), so the help will be below your full bill. Claim through your council.

What is left of your bill after 20p per £1 of income above your applicable amount — where your income is at or below it, that is the whole bill. And if the Pension Credit answer above applies, claim it first: it makes this maximum.
verdicts.rule: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 10 (classes A and B)

verdicts.because: Some of the bill appears covered after the 20% taper — but a deduction applies for each other adult living with you, sized by their income (not encoded here). Claim through your council and expect less than the working-out shown.

20p of each £1 of income above your applicable amount exceeds your weekly bill, so the taper reaches zero. Close cases are worth claiming anyway — and a Pension Credit award of even pennies would flip this to the whole bill.

not_asked: your address or council — the claim is your council's; the pension-age rules it must apply are national law, and those are what computed here
not_encoded: non-dependant deductions (Sch 1 para 8: a deduction per other adult in the home, sized by their income — where they apply, the flagged amount will be lower)
the second-adult rebate (class C: up to 25% where other adults on low incomes live with you — ask your council to work out both and give the higher)
councils may be MORE generous than these prescribed rules (LGFA 1992 s.13A(1)(c) discretion) — this file computes the national floor, never your council's ceiling
discounts and exemptions themselves (the 25% single-person discount, severe mental impairment, and others — enter your bill after them; if you have not claimed them, that is more money again)
Wales's default national scheme (a close cousin of these rules, not verified line by line here)

sources_checked.what: On guarantee credit the whole of capital and income must be disregarded (verbatim)
sources_checked.where: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 13 (legislation.gov.uk revised text) — cross-checked against Age UK ('you may even get your Council Tax paid in full')

sources_checked.what: The taper: class B deducts 2 6/7 per cent of the weekly excess against the daily liability — exactly 20 per cent in weekly terms
Tariff income £1 per £500 (or part) above £10,000, up to the £16,000 ceiling (verbatim)
sources_checked.where: SI 2012/2885 Sch 1 para 37 (legislation.gov.uk revised text) — £16,000 limit cross-checked against Age UK

sources_checked.what: Applicable amounts £238.00 / £363.25; severe disability premium £86.05 / £172.10; carer premium £48.15 (April 2026, uprated by S.I. 2026/27)
Pensioner definition and the working-age/mixed-age boundary
